text.title: Methodology Contribution Flow (Open Authorship) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Methodology Contribution Flow (Open Authorship) methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every methodology PR uses the canonical template: topic, tier rationale, prior-art search, draft AGENTS.md + content/01..03 + content/06, evidence sources, attribution preference. Missing sections block merge.
rule.rationale: Template removes drafting friction and forces evidence + tier rationale up-front; missing fields are the dominant cause of bounce-back.

rule.statement: Three review axes (a) v2 shape compliance, (b) ≥3 sources cited per major claim, (c) no duplication with existing entries — each axis MUST be reviewed by a different maintainer. Two-reviewer or single-axis reviews fail.
rule.rationale: Three-axis × three-maintainer review prevents single-reviewer bias and misses duplication; observed accuracy lift in audited PRs.

rule.statement: Author retains attribution: name + URL in `maintainers` list of the merged methodology AND in CHANGELOG. Contribution flows under the linked CLA which grants faion redistribution rights.
rule.rationale: Visible attribution motivates contribution and builds contributor brand; CLA enables redistribution without renegotiation.

rule.statement: ≥5 accepted contributions trigger revenue-share enrollment: 10% of CLI-paid-tier revenue attributed to those methodologies (via tier-manifest), paid quarterly, audited yearly.
rule.rationale: Compensation aligns long-term contribution with revenue; documented policy avoids ad-hoc favouritism.

rule.statement: Contributor methodologies enter the standard 12-month review cycle. Contributor is invited to refresh; if declined, an internal maintainer takes over and updates `maintainers` accordingly.
rule.rationale: Same decay rule as internal entries; ensures consistency and prevents zombie content.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-methodology-contribution-flow-open-authorship.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
